name: DGL (Deep Graph Library)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache-2.0); Amazon/AWS and academic contributors

performance.notes: Framework-agnostic (PyTorch/TensorFlow/JAX); optimized sparse operations; mini-batch sampling

agent_readiness.notes: Framework-agnostic GNN library (PyTorch, TensorFlow, JAX). import dgl. import torch. g = dgl.graph(([0,1], [1,2])). g.ndata['feat'] = torch.rand(3, 16). from dgl.nn import GraphConv. class GCN(nn.Module): def __init__(self): self.conv = GraphConv(16, 32). def forward(self, g, feat): return self.conv(g, feat). Message passing API: g.update_all(dgl.function.copy_u('h','m'), dgl.function.mean('m','h')). Heterogeneous graphs: dgl.heterograph(). OGB integration. Distributed training. DGL-LifeSci for molecular ML. Less opinionated than PyG — lower-level message passing control.

gotchas: More verbose than PyTorch Geometric for standard GNN layers
Multi-framework support adds API surface complexity
Heterogeneous graph API is complex to learn
Smaller community than PyG in recent years
best_when: TensorFlow or JAX GNN training (PyG is PyTorch-only)
Custom message passing algorithms needing low-level control
Distributed GNN training on large graphs
avoid_when: PyTorch-only teams — PyG has larger community and ecosystem
Standard GNN layers (GCN/GAT) — PyG has simpler API
summary: DGL (Deep Graph Library) is a framework-agnostic GNN library (PyTorch, TensorFlow, JAX). g = dgl.graph(src, dst). g.ndata['feat'] = features. Low-level message passing API: g.update_all(copy_u, mean). GraphConv, GATConv, SAGEConv layers. Heterogeneous graphs via dgl.heterograph(). Distributed training. DGL-LifeSci for molecular ML. More verbose than PyG but supports multiple frameworks and lower-level control over message passing.
